meaning: adj.[亦作moveable] 活动的,变动的
examples: It's a vinyl doll with movable arms and legs.
The new age of machinery, in short, could not have been born without a new source of both movable and constant power.
The Earth's crust is thought to be divided into huge, movable segments, called plates, which float on a soft plastic layer of rock.
It had a movable mechanical arm with a slender proboscis-like pipette at the tip.
Interior walls can be either movable and modular or easily constructed bulwarks of studs and drywall.
